contract.title: Will the Federal Reserve Cut rates by 25bps at their January 2028 meeting?
contract.subtitle: Cut 25bps
contract.resolution_criteria: If the Federal Reserve does a Cut of 25bps on January 26, 2028, then the market resolves to Yes.

conditions.label: FOMC 25 bps cut on Jan 26, 2028
conditions.summary: On Jan 26, 2028, the FOMC announces an exact 25 bp reduction in the federal funds rate target range (both bounds down 0.25 pp vs prior), per official Federal Reserve release.

contract.narrative: Sanctions-led energy pressures dominate: repeated OFAC hits on Iran- and Iraq-linked oil trade add inflation risk, tilting odds away from a 25 bps cut, while tax-cut rhetoric and Bowman’s hawkish tone reinforce caution. Capital/leverage rule tweaks are background. Waller’s pro-cuts case offers a mild counter, but the call still awaits inflation/oil trends and upcoming FOMC signals into Jan 2028.

timeline.title: Economic Fury Ramps Up Pressure on Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Oil Operations
timeline.regulator: Department of the Treasury — Office of Foreign Assets Control
timeline.one_line_why: OFAC sanctions on Iran's oil sector could tighten supply, raise oil-driven inflation, and thus reduce the likelihood of a Fed cut in Jan 2028.

timeline.title: Economic Fury Targets Iraqi Oil Official, Iran-Backed Terrorist Militias in Iraq
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control — Office of Foreign Assets Control
timeline.one_line_why: OFAC sanctions on Iraqi oil entities could tighten supply and lift oil-driven inflation, modestly lowering odds of a Fed 25bp cut by Jan 2028.

timeline.title: 2026-04-30 - New Jersey Employment Report: March 2026
timeline.regulator: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development — Office of Research and Information
timeline.one_line_why: A single-state employment report is an indirect, minor macro signal for FOMC policy but gives no clear directional info toward a Jan 2028 cut.

timeline.title: Economic Fury Targets Global Network Fueling Iran’s Oil Trade and Shadow Fleet
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control
timeline.one_line_why: OFAC sanctions on Iran-linked oil trade could tighten supply and lift inflation, making a Jan 2028 25bp Fed cut less likely (bearish, low impact).

timeline.title: Agencies Finalize Changes to Community Bank Leverage Ratio
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: Finalized community-bank leverage-rule changes are a background regulatory tweak with minimal indirect effect on odds of a Jan 26, 2028 25bp Fed cut.

timeline.title: Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: Final OCC rule on community bank leverage ratio affects bank capital and credit supply only indirectly, offering minimal signal about a Jan 26, 2028 Fed rate cut.

timeline.title: Over 53 Million Filers Claimed At Least One of President Trump’s Signature New Tax Cuts
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury
timeline.one_line_why: Widespread tax cuts likely boost demand and inflation, lowering the chance of a Jan 2028 25bp Fed cut (small negative signal).

timeline.title: Stephen I Miran: Prospects for shrinking the Federal Reserve Bank's balance sheet
timeline.regulator: Bank for International Settlements
timeline.one_line_why: BIS analysis arguing for Fed balance-sheet shrinkage implies a sustained tightening bias, slightly decreasing odds of a 25bp Jan 2028 cut.
timeline.effective_date: some time after decision to proceed, likely well over a year and possibly several years

timeline.title: Regulatory Capital Rule: Category I and II Banking Organizations, Banking Organizations with Significant Trading Activity, and Optional Adoption for Other Banking Organizations
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: FDIC capital-rule affects bank resilience and lending conditions, an indirect and small influence on Fed policy but not directly determinative of a Jan‑26, 2028 25bp cut.

timeline.title: Agencies Request Comment on Proposals to Modernize the Regulatory Capital Framework And Maintain the Strength of the Banking System
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: Regulatory capital proposals are long‑term, indirect signals for monetary policy and provide only a weak, ambiguous influence on a Jan 2028 Fed cut.

timeline.title: Regulatory Capital: Category I and II Banking Organizations, Banking Organizations With Significant Trading Activity, and Optional Adoption for Other Banking Organizations
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: Banking capital rule proposal affects financial conditions indirectly and only modestly, so it offers little direct information on a Jan 26, 2028 25bp Fed cut.

timeline.title: Regulatory Capital: Standardized Approach for Risk-Weighted Assets
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: OCC capital-rule affects bank capital and lending only indirectly; it’s a background macroprudential signal unlikely to materially shift odds of a Jan 26, 2028 25bp Fed cut.

timeline.title: Isabel Schnabel: Navigating inflation and employment in an era of supply shocks and AI
timeline.regulator: Bank for International Settlements
timeline.one_line_why: BIS/ECB analysis on inflation, employment and AI offers background on forces shaping Fed policy but doesn't materially change Jan-2028 cut odds.

timeline.title: President Trump’s Tax Cuts are Putting More Money Back into the Pockets of American Families and Workers
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury
timeline.one_line_why: Treasury's 2026 claim that Trump's tax cuts boost household income could raise demand/inflation, making a 25bps Fed cut in Jan 2028 slightly less likely.

timeline.title: Christopher J Waller: Labor market data - signal or noise?
timeline.regulator: Bank for International Settlements
timeline.one_line_why: BIS piece on interpreting U.S. labor-market data is background commentary affecting Fed views; indirect, ambiguous effect on likelihood of a Jan 2028 25bp cut.

timeline.title: Michelle W Bowman: Outlook for the economy and monetary policy
timeline.regulator: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System — supervision
timeline.one_line_why: Bowman’s Feb 2026 policy outlook signals persistent hawkishness, reducing the chance a 25bp cut will occur at the Jan 26, 2028 meeting.

timeline.title: Agencies Issue Final Rule to Modify Certain Regulatory Capital Standards
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: Changes to bank capital rules could slightly affect lending and macro conditions that influence Fed cuts, but lacking specifics the effect and direction are unclear.

timeline.title: Modifications to the Enhanced Supplementary Leverage Ratio Standards for U.S. Global Systemically Important Bank Holding Companies and Their Subsidiary Depository Institutions: Final Rule
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: Background OCC final rule changing GSIB eSLR standards; may marginally affect financial conditions but gives no clear signal on a Jan 26, 2028 25bp Fed cut.

timeline.title: Christopher J Waller: The case for continuing rate cuts
timeline.regulator: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
timeline.one_line_why: Waller's Nov 2025 speech urging continued rate cuts modestly raises the probability the Fed eases through 2028, making a Jan 26, 2028 cut slightly more likely.

timeline.title: Treasury Tightens Sanctions on Iran’s Oil Network Supporting its Military
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury — Office of Foreign Assets Control
timeline.one_line_why: US sanctions on Iran's oil network could tighten supply and lift oil-driven inflation, making a Jan 2028 Fed cut slightly less likely.

primary_drivers: Economic Fury campaign disrupting Iran’s oil trade and shadow fleet
Targeted actions against Iraqi oil officials and Iran-backed militias
U.S. banking rulemaking: community-bank leverage ratio final rule and capital modernization proposals
heat_panel.explainer: Intense 'Economic Fury' actions targeting Iran-linked oil networks and shadow fleets, plus major U.S. banking rulemaking (community-bank leverage ratio final rule and capital modernization proposals), are keeping heat critical despite recent cooling.
